title: Reptonia mascatensis
taxonomy: Reptonia mascatensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the Socotra Archipelago in Yemen. The plant is classified as part of the genus Reptonia, which is a small genus of succulent plants. The classification of Reptonia mascatensis is further detailed in the APG III system of plant taxonomy. The APG III system assigns it to the clade of Angiosperms.

intro: Reptonia mascatensis, also known as Mascatia or Mascatan, is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, traditionally used in Oman and other parts of the Middle East for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Reptonia mascatensis include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
